annotations.label: Pentagon

anchor.snippet: is any five-sided polygon or 5-gon

annotations.note: Mathlib has a generic `Polygon P n` structure for arbitrary `n`, but no specialization to `n = 5` for a pentagon.

annotations.label: Sum of internal angles of a simple pentagon

anchor.snippet: The sum of the internal angles in a simple pentagon is 540°

annotations.note: No interior-angle-sum result for polygons (let alone for pentagons) appears in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Pentagram (self-intersecting regular pentagon)

anchor.snippet: A self-intersecting regular pentagon (or star pentagon ) is called a pentagram

annotations.note: Grep finds no `Pentagram`/`pentagram` definition in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Regular pentagon (Schläfli symbol and interior angles)

anchor.snippet: A regular pentagon has Schläfli symbol {5} and interior angles of 108°

annotations.note: Mathlib has no regular-polygon definition, no Schläfli-symbol notation, and no 108° interior-angle result.

annotations.label: Symmetries of the regular pentagon

anchor.snippet: It also has five lines of reflectional symmetry , and rotational symmetry of order 5

annotations.note: The abstract dihedral group `DihedralGroup 5` exists, but Mathlib does not formalize the regular pentagon nor identify its symmetry group with `DihedralGroup 5`.

annotations.label: Diagonals of a convex regular pentagon are in golden ratio to sides

anchor.snippet: The diagonals of a convex regular pentagon are in the golden ratio to its sides

annotations.note: The golden ratio constant is defined in `Mathlib.NumberTheory.Real.GoldenRatio`, but its relation to the regular pentagon's diagonal-to-side ratio is not formalized.

annotations.label: Height, width, diagonal, and circumradius formulas in terms of side length

anchor.snippet: width [MATH] (distance between two farthest separated points, which equals the diagonal length [MATH] ) and circumradius [MATH] are given by:

annotations.note: Mathlib has no regular pentagon and no formulas for its height, width, diagonal, or circumradius.

annotations.label: Area of a convex regular pentagon by side length

anchor.snippet: The area of a convex regular pentagon with side length

annotations.note: No regular-pentagon area formula is present in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Edge length from circumradius

anchor.snippet: If the circumradius [MATH] of a regular pentagon is given, its edge length [MATH] is found by the expression

annotations.note: No regular-pentagon edge-length-from-circumradius formula is present in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Fill ratio of regular pentagon in circumscribed circle

anchor.snippet: the regular pentagon fills approximately 0.7568 of its circumscribed circle

annotations.note: No regular-pentagon-to-circumscribed-circle fill ratio appears in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Area of a regular polygon formula

anchor.snippet: The area of any regular polygon is:

annotations.note: Mathlib lacks a general regular-polygon area formula (no regular polygon definition exists).

annotations.label: Area of regular pentagon via perimeter and inradius

anchor.snippet: Substituting the regular pentagon's values for P and r gives the formula

annotations.note: No P·r/2 area formula specialized to a regular pentagon is in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Inradius (apothem) of regular pentagon

anchor.snippet: The apothem , which is the radius r of the inscribed circle, of a regular pentagon is related to the side length t by

annotations.note: Grep for `apothem` finds nothing in Mathlib and no regular-pentagon inradius formula exists.

annotations.label: Chord sum identity on circumcircle of regular pentagon

anchor.section: Chords from the circumscribed circle to the vertices
anchor.snippet: if P is any point on the circumcircle between points B and C, then PA + PD = PB + PC + PE

annotations.note: This pentagon-specific chord identity is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Distance identity for arbitrary point in plane of regular pentagon

anchor.snippet: For an arbitrary point in the plane of a regular pentagon with circumradius

annotations.note: No such regular-pentagon distance identity exists in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Distance identity for point on circumcircle

anchor.snippet: If [MATH] are the distances from the vertices of a regular pentagon to any point on its circumcircle, then

annotations.note: No circumcircle distance identity for the regular pentagon's vertices is in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Regular pentagon is constructible with compass and straightedge

anchor.snippet: The regular pentagon is constructible with compass and straightedge , as 5 is a Fermat prime

annotations.note: Mathlib has no compass-and-straightedge constructibility predicate and no regular pentagon to apply it to.

annotations.label: Richmond's construction of the regular pentagon

anchor.snippet: One method to construct a regular pentagon in a given circle is described by Richmond

annotations.note: Richmond's construction is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Carlyle circles construction of the regular pentagon

anchor.snippet: This methodology leads to a procedure for constructing a regular pentagon

annotations.note: Carlyle circles and the resulting pentagon construction are absent from Mathlib.

annotations.label: Trigonometric construction of regular pentagon

anchor.snippet: we can construct a regular pentagon by constructing a specific right triangle and duplicating it

annotations.note: The trigonometric construction of the regular pentagon is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Euclid's construction of the regular pentagon

anchor.snippet: A regular pentagon is constructible using a compass and straightedge , either by inscribing one in a given circle or constructing one on a given edge

annotations.note: Euclid's pentagon construction is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Paper-strip knot construction of regular pentagon

anchor.snippet: A regular pentagon may be created from just a strip of paper by tying an overhand knot

annotations.note: Physical paper-knot construction has no Mathlib counterpart.

annotations.label: Hexagon-folding construction of pentagonal pyramid base

anchor.snippet: Construct a regular hexagon on stiff paper or card

annotations.note: Physical hexagon-folding construction has no Mathlib counterpart.

annotations.label: Symmetry group of regular pentagon

anchor.snippet: The regular pentagon has Dih 5 symmetry , order 10

annotations.note: `DihedralGroup 5` of order 10 exists in Mathlib but is not connected to a (nonexistent) regular pentagon.

annotations.label: Pentagram (regular star pentagon)

anchor.snippet: A pentagram or pentangle is a regular star pentagon

annotations.note: No pentagram/star polygon definition is found in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Sides of pentagram form diagonals of regular convex pentagon in golden ratio

anchor.snippet: Its sides form the diagonals of a regular convex pentagon

annotations.note: Neither pentagram nor its relation to regular pentagon diagonals is in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Equilateral pentagon

anchor.snippet: An equilateral pentagon is a polygon with five sides of equal length

annotations.note: No equilateral-pentagon (or general equilateral-polygon) predicate is present in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Uniqueness of the regular pentagon up to similarity

anchor.snippet: the regular pentagon is unique up to similarity, because it is equilateral and it is equiangular

annotations.note: Uniqueness up to similarity for the regular pentagon is not in Mathlib (no regular pentagon definition exists).

annotations.label: Cyclic pentagon

anchor.snippet: A cyclic pentagon is one for which a circle called the circumcircle goes through all five vertices

annotations.note: Mathlib has circumcenters of simplices but no cyclic-pentagon / cyclic-polygon predicate.

annotations.label: Area of cyclic pentagon as root of septic equation

anchor.snippet: The area of a cyclic pentagon, whether regular or not, can be expressed as one fourth the square root of one of the roots of a septic equation

annotations.note: Robbins's septic for the cyclic-pentagon area is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Robbins pentagons

anchor.snippet: There exist cyclic pentagons with rational sides and rational area; these are called Robbins pentagons

annotations.note: Robbins pentagons are not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Diagonals of a Robbins pentagon all rational or all irrational

anchor.snippet: the diagonals of a Robbins pentagon must be either all rational or all irrational

annotations.note: This Robbins-pentagon diagonal dichotomy is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Inequality for sides and diagonals of convex pentagons

anchor.snippet: For all convex pentagons with sides

annotations.note: No convex-pentagon side-diagonal inequality appears in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Regular pentagon cannot tile regularly

anchor.snippet: A regular pentagon cannot appear in any tiling of regular polygons

annotations.note: Mathlib's tiling framework (`Mathlib.Combinatorics.Tiling.Tile`) is abstract and does not contain this regular-pentagon non-tiling result.

annotations.label: Optimal packing density of regular pentagons (Hales–Kusner)

anchor.snippet: The maximum known packing density of a regular pentagon is

annotations.note: The Hales–Kusner regular-pentagon packing density is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Fifteen classes of pentagons that monohedrally tile the plane

anchor.snippet: There are 15 classes of pentagons that can monohedrally tile the plane

annotations.note: The classification of the 15 monohedral pentagon tilings is not formalized in Mathlib.
